description_long: This playground object consists of two adjacent swing sets mounted on a rectangular paved surface. The left structure features a traditional flat seat swing suspended by metal chains from a wooden frame, while the right structure has a circular basket or nest swing, also suspended by chains from a similar wooden frame. The supporting posts and crossbeams are made of light-colored wood, giving the equipment a natural and sturdy appearance. The swing seats are constructed from black materials, with the basket swing having a mesh-like design for group or inclusive play. The entire setup is designed for outdoor use, likely in a park or urban recreational area, and does not include any visible safety barriers or shade structures. The ground surface beneath the swings is flat and appears to be made of concrete or a similar hard material.
description: A dual swing set with one traditional flat swing and one circular basket swing, both suspended from wooden frames on a paved surface. The design is simple, sturdy, and suitable for urban playgrounds.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the flat seat swing on the left and the circular basket swing on the right, clearly displaying the wooden frames and chain suspensions.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the swing frames and the circular profile of the basket swing, with both swings aligned in a row.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the backs of both swings and the supporting wooden structures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the swing frames' side profiles and the arrangement of the swings.
